health_signal_policy.label: minimum hygiene signal
health_signal_policy.summary: Health grades are triage signals for code hygiene. They do not prove product readiness.
limitations: Health scores do not prove real user workflow quality.
Health scores do not prove enterprise deployment readiness.
Health scores do not prove security control effectiveness beyond the scanned checks.
Health scores do not prove market positioning, AI citation, or customer trust.

gates.title: Flyto2 Cloud / Apps / Automation evidence
gates.confidence: No-code automation readiness is supported by workspace inventory, architecture mapping, state-machine guards, deterministic product verification, and browser smoke evidence.

gates.title: Flyto2 Security evidence
gates.confidence: Security readiness is supported by entitlement/RBAC, tenant isolation, state-machine, deterministic product verification, security/CI evidence, and proof that required GitHub Actions workflows started, created jobs, and passed.

gates.title: Flyto2 Data architecture reservation evidence
gates.confidence: The future Data line is not claimed as feature-complete; release confidence requires explicit architecture and ownership reservation evidence.

gates.title: Flyto2 Zero-person Company Agent evidence
gates.confidence: Agent OS readiness is supported by runtime architecture, state handling, deterministic product verification, browser smoke, and security/operability evidence.

gates.title: Flyto2 Big Data / Intelligence evidence
gates.confidence: Intelligence readiness is supported by GEO/AI crawler evidence, public-site reachability verification, architecture mapping, and security/performance evidence.

gates.title: Enterprise / airgap deployment evidence
gates.confidence: Enterprise readiness requires airgap/open-core boundary evidence, CI/security evidence, and proof that required GitHub Actions workflows started, created jobs, and passed.

gates.title: SEO / AEO / GEO visibility evidence
gates.confidence: AI/search visibility confidence requires crawler/GEO evidence, public-site route/browser verification, and multilingual/i18n evidence.

gates.title: Release operations evidence
gates.confidence: Production release confidence requires a passing product gate, clean inventory, deterministic product verification, public-site verification, CI/security evidence, and proof that required GitHub Actions workflows started, created jobs, and passed.
